title: To E---[1]
content:
Let Folly smile, to view the names
    Of thee and me, in Friendship twin'd;
    Yet Virtue will have greater claims
    To love, than rank with vice combin'd.

    And though unequal is thy fate,
    Since title deck'd my higher birth;
    Yet envy not this gaudy state,
    Thine is the pride of modest worth.

    Our souls at least congenial meet,
    Nor can thy lot my rank disgrace;
    Our intercourse is not less sweet,
    Since worth of rank supplies the place.
meaning:
George Gordon Byron's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "To E---[1]"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
